component_identification.content: Concordia features a Mediterranean map board, city demand tiles, goods tokens (brick, food, tool, wine, cloth), houses for each player, money, personality cards (starting deck plus market), prefect tokens, bonus markers, and scoring reference cards for gods like Jupiter, Saturnus, Mercurius, Minerva, Mars, and Vesta.

core_game_loop.content: On your turn, play one card from hand and resolve its effect. Cards drive all actions: move colonists, build houses, produce goods, trade, copy effects, acquire new cards, or retrieve your hand with Tribune. The game is a hand-cycling economy race where map expansion and card purchases both feed final scoring multipliers.

detailed_rules.content: Movement via Architect spends movement points along routes and allows building in cities where you have presence, paying costs in goods and coins. Prefect either produces in one province for all players (taking province bonus) or claims coin bonuses from already-prefected provinces via the alternate side. Mercator gives flexible money and trade conversion. Senator/Consul purchase new cards from the market row, adding powers and scoring potential. Diplomat copies the last played card of another player, creating tactical timing plays. Tribune recalls your discard to hand, grants coins based on cards recalled, and allows optional colonist expansion. Endgame is triggered when personality cards run low or one player places most houses. Scoring is delayed and based on gods tied to card counts and board achievements, so visible VP during play is minimal.

scoring_and_endgame.content: Each personality card scores by its god condition: Jupiter for houses in non-brick cities, Saturnus for provinces occupied, Mars for colonists, Mercurius for goods diversity, Minerva for specialized city types, and Vesta for cash thresholds. Multiply your board state by how many of each god card you own. Highest score wins; ties typically break by remaining cash and goods.

beginner_strategy.content: Do not evaluate cards only by action text; scoring symbols are equally important. Expand to multiple provinces early so Prefect production stays strong. Keep Tribune timing efficient—cycling too slowly wastes turns, but recalling too early reduces action density. Buy cards that match your map footprint and intended scoring gods instead of grabbing generic value. Maintain cash buffer so Architect turns can convert movement into actual builds.
